skills.label: SELECT
examples: SELECT name, department FROM employees;
SELECT * FROM employees;

skills.label: WHERE
skills.summary: Filter rows that match a condition.
examples: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE salary > 70000;
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales';

skills.label: ORDER BY
skills.summary: Sort result rows by one or more columns.
skills.syntax: SELECT … FROM table ORDER BY column [ASC|DESC];
examples: SELECT name, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary;
SELECT name FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC;

skills.label: LIMIT
skills.summary: Return only the first N rows.
examples: SELECT name FROM employees LIMIT 3;
SELECT name FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 2;

skills.label: LIKE
skills.summary: Match text patterns with wildcards (% and _).
examples: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE name LIKE '%Chen%';
SELECT name FROM employees WHERE name LIKE 'A%';

skills.label: IN
skills.summary: Match a value against a list.
examples: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE department IN ('Engineering', 'Sales');

skills.label: INSERT
skills.summary: Add a new row to a table.
skills.syntax: INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (val1, val2);
examples: INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department, salary, hired_date) VALUES (7, 'Grace Park', 'Engineering', 90000, '2024-01-15');

skills.label: UPDATE
skills.syntax: UPDATE table SET column = value WHERE condition;
examples: UPDATE employees SET salary = 70000 WHERE name = 'David Kim';

skills.label: DELETE
skills.summary: Remove rows that match a condition.
examples: DELETE FROM employees WHERE name = 'Frank Lee';

skills.label: COUNT
examples: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales';

skills.label: SUM
skills.summary: Add up numeric values in a column.
examples: SELECT SUM(salary) FROM employees;

skills.label: AVG
skills.summary: Compute the average of numeric values.
examples: SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees WHERE department = 'Engineering';

skills.label: MIN/MAX
skills.summary: Find the smallest or largest value in a column.
examples: SELECT MIN(salary), MAX(salary) FROM employees;

skills.label: GROUP BY
skills.summary: Collapse rows into groups and aggregate each group.
skills.syntax: SELECT col, AGG(…) FROM table GROUP BY col;
examples: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY department;

skills.label: HAVING
skills.summary: Filter groups after aggregation (like WHERE for groups).
examples: SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING AVG(salary) > 70000;

skills.label: Aliases
skills.summary: Rename columns (or tables) in the result.
skills.syntax: SELECT column AS alias_name FROM table;
examples: SELECT name AS employee_name, salary AS yearly_pay FROM employees;

skills.label: JOIN
skills.syntax: SELECT … FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id;
examples: SELECT e.name, d.budget FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department = d.name;

skills.label: CREATE TABLE
skills.summary: Define a new table and its columns.
examples: CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, budget REAL);

skills.label: ALTER TABLE
skills.syntax: ALTER TABLE name ADD COLUMN col type;
examples: ALTER TABLE employees ADD COLUMN email TEXT;

upgrades.label: Second extra slot
upgrades.description: +1 more inbox slot (needs Extra inbox slot).

upgrades.label: Hint tokens (×3)
upgrades.description: Buy 3 soft hints for the active ticket.

tickets.title: What's on the menu?
tickets.prompt: List every drink name and price.

tickets.title: Just the names
tickets.prompt: Return only the drink names.

tickets.title: Drinks over $4
tickets.prompt: Show drinks that cost more than 4 dollars (name and price).

validation.query: SELECT name, price FROM drinks WHERE price > 4

tickets.title: Espresso category
tickets.prompt: List name and price for drinks in the espresso category.

validation.query: SELECT name, price FROM drinks WHERE category = 'espresso'

tickets.title: Cheapest first
tickets.prompt: List all drink names and prices, cheapest first.

validation.query: SELECT name, price FROM drinks ORDER BY price

tickets.title: Price high to low
tickets.prompt: List drink names ordered by price descending.

validation.query: SELECT name FROM drinks ORDER BY price DESC

tickets.title: Top of the menu
tickets.prompt: Show the first 3 drinks by id (name only).
tickets.hint: ORDER BY id LIMIT 3 — or LIMIT 3 if ids are ordered.

validation.query: SELECT name FROM drinks ORDER BY id LIMIT 3

tickets.title: Two cheapest
tickets.prompt: Return the 2 cheapest drink names (cheapest first).

validation.query: SELECT name FROM drinks ORDER BY price LIMIT 2

tickets.title: Anything with latte
tickets.prompt: Find drinks whose name contains the word latte (case-insensitive is fine if you use LIKE '%latte%' on these names).

validation.query: SELECT name, price FROM drinks WHERE name LIKE '%Latte%'

tickets.title: Espresso or tea
tickets.prompt: List drinks in category espresso or tea (name, category, price).

validation.query: SELECT name, category, price FROM drinks WHERE category IN ('espresso', 'tea')

tickets.title: Add Matcha
tickets.prompt: Insert a new drink: Matcha, category tea, price 4.50. (If Matcha Latte already exists, add a distinct row named exactly Matcha.)
tickets.hint: INSERT INTO drinks (id, name, category, price) VALUES (11, 'Matcha', 'tea', 4.50);

validation.verifyQuery: SELECT name, category, price FROM drinks WHERE name = 'Matcha'
validation.expectedQuery: SELECT 'Matcha' AS name, 'tea' AS category, 4.50 AS price

tickets.title: Cold Brew price change
tickets.prompt: Update Cold Brew so its price is 3.75.
tickets.hint: UPDATE drinks SET price = 3.75 WHERE name = 'Cold Brew';

validation.verifyQuery: SELECT price FROM drinks WHERE name = 'Cold Brew'

tickets.title: Remove Pumpkin Spice
tickets.prompt: Delete the Pumpkin Spice drink from the menu.
tickets.hint: DELETE FROM drinks WHERE name = 'Pumpkin Spice';

validation.verifyQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM drinks WHERE name = 'Pumpkin Spice'

tickets.title: How many drinks?
tickets.prompt: Return a single count of all drinks.

tickets.title: Sum of prices
tickets.prompt: Return the sum of all drink prices.

tickets.title: Average espresso price
tickets.prompt: Average price of drinks in the espresso category.
tickets.hint: SELECT AVG(price) FROM drinks WHERE category = 'espresso';

validation.query: SELECT AVG(price) FROM drinks WHERE category = 'espresso'

tickets.title: Price range
tickets.prompt: Return the minimum and maximum drink prices.

tickets.title: Count by category
tickets.prompt: For each category, show the category and how many drinks it has.

validation.query: SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM drinks GROUP BY category

tickets.title: Pricey categories
tickets.prompt: Categories whose average price is greater than 4 (category and avg price).

validation.query: SELECT category, AVG(price) FROM drinks GROUP BY category HAVING AVG(price) > 4

tickets.title: Friendly column names
tickets.prompt: Select drink name as drink_name and price as menu_price.
tickets.hint: SELECT name AS drink_name, price AS menu_price FROM drinks;

validation.query: SELECT name AS drink_name, price AS menu_price FROM drinks

tickets.title: Who ordered what?
tickets.prompt: List customer name, drink name, and qty for every order.
tickets.hint: JOIN customers and drinks through orders.

validation.query: SELECT c.name AS customer, d.name AS drink, o.qty FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN drinks d ON o.drink_id = d.id

tickets.title: Loyalty table
tickets.prompt: Create a table loyalty_members with columns id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY) and email (TEXT NOT NULL).
tickets.hint: CREATE TABLE loyalty_members (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, email TEXT NOT NULL);

validation.verifyQuery: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'table' AND name = 'loyalty_members'

tickets.title: Add caffeine column
tickets.prompt: Add an INTEGER column caffeine_mg to the drinks table.
tickets.hint: ALTER TABLE drinks ADD COLUMN caffeine_mg INTEGER;

validation.verifyQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM pragma_table_info('drinks') WHERE name = 'caffeine_mg'

tickets.title: Over $4, cheapest first
tickets.prompt: Drinks costing more than 4, cheapest first (name and price).

validation.query: SELECT name, price FROM drinks WHERE price > 4 ORDER BY price
